name: Deadband Observatory
description: You step into a domed observatory where the air is perfectly still — so still you can feel your own pulse. The dome is transparent above, revealing a mathematical sky where precision itself is visible as luminous filaments. A massive funnel dominates the center of the room, its walls converging toward an infinitesimally small point at the bottom. This is the precision funnel — where noise becomes signal.
look_text: The observatory is organized by perceptual depth. Near the door: the concept of deadband — the region where change is imperceptible. Center: the precision funnel itself, where measurement resolution narrows until only the true signal remains. Far alcove: proprioception experiments — feeling the exact moment when drift becomes detectable. A large clock on the wall counts down from T-minus, measuring the approach to convergence.

exits.description: The lattice gallery above, where hexagons tile everything
The cathedral below, where XOR reveals hidden structure
The temple beyond, where cycles achieve coherence

recipes.name: deadband detector
recipes.description: An algorithm that determines the exact deadband threshold for any computational system — the boundary between 'no perceptible change' and 'drift detected'.

recipes.name: precision funnel model
recipes.description: A mathematical model of the precision funnel: how measurement noise narrows as observations accumulate, converging on the true value with quantifiable certainty.
